verses.latin_vulgate: Et accidit quadam die ut diceret Jonathas filius Saul ad adolescentem armigerum suum : Veni, et transeamus ad stationem Philisthinorum, quæ est trans locum illum. Patri autem suo hoc ipsum non indicavit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְהִ֣י הַיּ֗וֹם וַיֹּ֨אמֶר יוֹנָתָ֤ן בֶּן־שָׁאוּל֙ אֶל־הַנַּ֙עַר֙ נֹשֵׂ֣א כֵלָ֔יו לְכָ֗ה וְנַעְבְּרָה֙ אֶל־מַצַּ֣ב פְּלִשְׁתִּ֔ים אֲשֶׁ֖ר מֵעֵ֣בֶר הַלָּ֑ז וּלְאָבִ֖יו לֹ֥א הִגִּֽיד׃
verses.douay_rheims: Now it came to pass one day that Jonathan, the son of Saul, said to the young man that bore his armour: Come, and let us go over to the garrison of the Philistines, which is on the other side of yonder place. But he told not this to his father.
verses.finnish_biblia: Se tapahtui yhtenä päivänä, että Jonatan Saulin poika sanoi palveliallensa, joka kantoi hänen asettansa: tule, käykäämme Philistealaisten leiriin, jotka toisella puolella ovat; ja ei hän sitä sanonut isällensä.
verses.king_james: Now it came to pass upon a day, that Jonathan the son of Saul said unto the young man that bare his armour, Come, and let us go over to the Philistines’ garrison, that is on the other side. But he told not his father.

verses.latin_vulgate: Porro Saul morabatur in extrema parte Gabaa sub malogranato, quæ erat in Magron : et erat populus cum eo quasi sexcentorum virorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְשָׁא֗וּל יוֹשֵׁב֙ בִּקְצֵ֣ה הַגִּבְעָ֔ה תַּ֥חַת הָרִמּ֖וֹן אֲשֶׁ֣ר בְּמִגְר֑וֹן וְהָעָם֙ אֲשֶׁ֣ר עִמּ֔וֹ כְּשֵׁ֥שׁ מֵא֖וֹת אִֽישׁ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Saul abode in the uttermost part of Gabaa, under the pomegranate tree, which was in Magron: and the people with him were about six hundred men.
verses.finnish_biblia: Vaan Saul viipyi Gibean ääressä granatin puun alla, joka oli esikaupungissa, ja se kansa, joka hänen tykönänsä oli, oli liki kuusisataa miestä.
verses.king_james: And Saul tarried in the uttermost part of Gibeah under a pomegranate tree which is in Migron: and the people that were with him were about six hundred men;

verses.latin_vulgate: Et Achias filius Achitob fratris Ichabod filii Phinees, qui ortus fuerat ex Heli sacerdote Domini in Silo, portabat ephod. Sed et populus ignorabat quo isset Jonathas.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַאֲחִיָּ֣ה בֶן־אֲחִט֡וּב אֲחִ֡י אִיכָב֣וֹד ׀ בֶּן־פִּֽינְחָ֨ס בֶּן־עֵלִ֜י כֹּהֵ֧ן ׀ יְהֹוָ֛ה בְּשִׁל֖וֹ נֹשֵׂ֣א אֵפ֑וֹד וְהָעָם֙ לֹ֣א יָדַ֔ע כִּ֥י הָלַ֖ךְ יוֹנָתָֽן׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Achias, the son of Achitob, brother of Ichabod the son of Phinees, the son of Heli, the priest of the Lord in Silo, wore the ephod. And the people knew not whither Jonathan was gone.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Ahia Ahitobin poika, Ikabodin veljen, Pinehaan pojan, Elin pojan, oli Herran pappi Silossa, kantain päällisvaatetta; mutta kansa ei tietänyt Jonatanin menneeksi pois.
verses.king_james: And Ahiah, the son of Ahitub, I–chabod’s brother, the son of Phinehas, the son of Eli, the Lord’s priest in Shiloh, wearing an ephod. And the people knew not that Jonathan was gone.

verses.latin_vulgate: Erant autem inter ascensus per quos nitebatur Jonathas transire ad stationem Philisthinorum, eminentes petræ ex utraque parte, et quasi in modum dentium scopuli hinc et inde prærupti : nomen uni Boses, et nomen alteri Sene :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּבֵ֣ין הַֽמַּעְבְּר֗וֹת אֲשֶׁ֨ר בִּקֵּ֤שׁ יֽוֹנָתָן֙ לַעֲבֹר֙ עַל־מַצַּ֣ב פְּלִשְׁתִּ֔ים שֵׁן־הַסֶּ֤לַע מֵהָעֵ֙בֶר֙ מִזֶּ֔ה וְשֵׁן־הַסֶּ֥לַע מֵהָעֵ֖בֶר מִזֶּ֑ה וְשֵׁ֤ם הָאֶחָד֙ בּוֹצֵ֔ץ וְשֵׁ֥ם הָאֶחָ֖ד סֶֽנֶּה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Now there were between the ascents, by which Jonathan sought to go over to the garrison of the Philistines, rocks standing up on both sides, and steep cliffs like teeth on the one side, and on the other, the name of the one was Boses, and the name of the other was Sene:
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja tiellä, kussa Jonatan pyysi ylitse mennä Philistealaisten leiriin, oli kaksi jyrkkää kalliota, yksi tällä puolella ja toinen toisella puolella: yksi kutsuttiin Botsets, toinen Sene.
verses.king_james: And between the passages, by which Jonathan sought to go over unto the Philistines’ garrison, there was a sharp rock on the one side, and a sharp rock on the other side: and the name of the one was Bozez, and the name of the other Seneh.

verses.latin_vulgate: unus scopulus prominens ad aquilonem ex adverso Machmas, et alter ad meridiem contra Gabaa.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַשֵּׁ֧ן הָאֶחָ֛ד מָצ֥וּק מִצָּפ֖וֹן מ֣וּל מִכְמָ֑שׂ וְהָאֶחָ֥ד מִנֶּ֖גֶב מ֥וּל גָּֽבַע׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: One rock stood out toward the north, over against Machmas, and the other to the south, over against Gabaa.
verses.finnish_biblia: Yksi oli Mikmaan päin pohjasta ja toinen etelästä Gabaan päin.
verses.king_james: The forefront of the one was situate northward over against Michmash, and the other southward over against Gibeah.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixit autem Jonathas ad adolescentem armigerum suum : Veni, transeamus ad stationem incircumcisorum horum, si forte faciat Dominus pro nobis : quia non est Domino difficile salvare, vel in multis, vel in paucis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֨אמֶר יְהוֹנָתָ֜ן אֶל־הַנַּ֣עַר ׀ נֹשֵׂ֣א כֵלָ֗יו לְכָה֙ וְנַעְבְּרָ֗ה אֶל־מַצַּב֙ הָעֲרֵלִ֣ים הָאֵ֔לֶּה אוּלַ֛י יַעֲשֶׂ֥ה יְהֹוָ֖ה לָ֑נוּ כִּ֣י אֵ֤ין לַֽיהֹוָה֙ מַעְצ֔וֹר לְהוֹשִׁ֥יעַ בְּרַ֖ב א֥וֹ בִמְעָֽט׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Jonathan said to the young man that bore his armour: Come, let us go over to the garrison of these uncircumcised, it may be the Lord will do for us: because it is easy for the Lord to save either by many, or by few.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Jonatan sanoi palvelialle, joka oli hänen aseensa kantaja: tule, käykäämme näiden ympärileikkaamattomain leiriin: taitaa tapahtua, Herra on jotakin toimittava meidän kauttamme, sillä ei ole Herralle työläs auttaa monen kautta elikkä harvain.
verses.king_james: And Jonathan said to the young man that bare his armour, Come, and let us go over unto the garrison of these uncircumcised: it may be that the Lord will work for us: for there is no restraint to the Lord to save by many or by few.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixitque ei armiger suus : Fac omnia quæ placent animo tuo : perge quo cupis, et ero tecum ubicumque volueris.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֤אמֶר לוֹ֙ נֹשֵׂ֣א כֵלָ֔יו עֲשֵׂ֖ה כׇּל־אֲשֶׁ֣ר בִּלְבָבֶ֑ךָ נְטֵ֣ה לָ֔ךְ הִנְנִ֥י עִמְּךָ֖ כִּלְבָבֶֽךָ׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: And his armourbearer said to him: Do all that pleaseth thy mind: go whither thou wilt, and I will be with thee wheresoever thou hast a mind.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin hänen aseensa kantaja vastasi häntä: tee kaikki, mitä sinun sydämessäs on: mene matkaan, katso, minä olen sinun kanssas, niinkuin sinun sydämes tahtoo.
verses.king_james: And his armourbearer said unto him, Do all that is in thine heart: turn thee; behold, I am with thee according to thy heart.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ait Jonathas : Ecce nos transimus ad viros istos. Cumque apparuerimus eis,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֙אמֶר֙ יְה֣וֹנָתָ֔ן הִנֵּ֛ה אֲנַ֥חְנוּ עֹבְרִ֖ים אֶל־הָאֲנָשִׁ֑ים וְנִגְלִ֖ינוּ אֲלֵיהֶֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Jonathan said: Behold we will go over to these men. And when we shall be seen by them,
verses.finnish_biblia: Jonatan sanoi: katso, me menemme niiden miesten tykö ja ilmoitamme itsemme heille.
verses.king_james: Then said Jonathan, Behold, we will pass over unto these men, and we will discover ourselves unto them.

verses.latin_vulgate: si taliter locuti fuerint ad nos : Manete donec veniamus ad vos : stemus in loco nostro, nec ascendamus ad eos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִם־כֹּ֤ה יֹֽאמְרוּ֙ אֵלֵ֔ינוּ דֹּ֕מּוּ עַד־הַגִּיעֵ֖נוּ אֲלֵיכֶ֑ם וְעָמַ֣דְנוּ תַחְתֵּ֔ינוּ וְלֹ֥א נַעֲלֶ֖ה אֲלֵיהֶֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: If they shall speak thus to us: Stay till we come to you: let us stand still in our place, and not go up to them.
verses.finnish_biblia: Jos he näin meille sanovat: seisokaat alallanne siihen asti että me tulemme teidän tykönne, niin seisokaamme siassamme ja älkäämme menkö heidän tykönsä.
verses.king_james: If they say thus unto us, Tarry until we come to you; then we will stand still in our place, and will not go up unto them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si autem dixerint : Ascendite ad nos : ascendamus, quia tradidit eos Dominus in manibus nostris : hoc erit nobis signum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאִם־כֹּ֨ה יֹאמְר֜וּ עֲל֤וּ עָלֵ֙ינוּ֙ וְעָלִ֔ינוּ כִּֽי־נְתָנָ֥ם יְהֹוָ֖ה בְּיָדֵ֑נוּ וְזֶה־לָּ֖נוּ הָאֽוֹת׃
verses.douay_rheims: But if they shall say: Come up to us: let us go up, because the Lord hath delivered them into our hands, this shall be a sign unto us.
verses.finnish_biblia: Vaan jos he näin sanovat: tulkaat tänne meidän tykömme, niin astukaamme heidän tykönsä; sillä Herra on antanut heidät meidän käsiimme: se olkoon meille merkiksi.
verses.king_james: But if they say thus, Come up unto us; then we will go up: for the Lord hath delivered them into our hand: and this shall be a sign unto us.

verses.latin_vulgate: Apparuit igitur uterque stationi Philisthinorum : dixeruntque Philisthiim : En Hebræi egrediuntur de cavernis in quibus absconditi fuerant.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּגָּל֣וּ שְׁנֵיהֶ֔ם אֶל־מַצַּ֖ב פְּלִשְׁתִּ֑ים וַיֹּאמְר֣וּ פְלִשְׁתִּ֔ים הִנֵּ֤ה עִבְרִים֙ יֹֽצְאִ֔ים מִן־הַחֹרִ֖ים אֲשֶׁ֥ר הִתְחַבְּאוּ־שָֽׁם׃
verses.douay_rheims: So both of them discovered themselves to the garrison of the Philistines: and the Philistines said: Behold the Hebrews come forth out of the holes wherein they were hid.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuin Philistealaisten leiri näki heidät molemmat, sanoivat Philistealaiset: katso, Hebrealaiset ovat lähteneet luolistansa, joihin he heitänsä lymyttäneet olivat.
verses.king_james: And both of them discovered themselves unto the garrison of the Philistines: and the Philistines said, Behold, the Hebrews come forth out of the holes where they had hid themselves.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et locuti sunt viri de statione ad Jonathan et ad armigerum ejus, dixeruntque : Ascendite ad nos, et ostendemus vobis rem. Et ait Jonathas ad armigerum suum : Ascendamus : sequere me : tradidit enim Dominus eos in manus Israël.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּעֲנוּ֩ אַנְשֵׁ֨י הַמַּצָּבָ֜ה אֶת־יוֹנָתָ֣ן ׀ וְאֶת־נֹשֵׂ֣א כֵלָ֗יו וַיֹּֽאמְרוּ֙ עֲל֣וּ אֵלֵ֔ינוּ וְנוֹדִ֥יעָה אֶתְכֶ֖ם דָּבָ֑ר {פ}וַיֹּ֨אמֶר יוֹנָתָ֜ן אֶל־נֹשֵׂ֤א כֵלָיו֙ עֲלֵ֣ה אַחֲרַ֔י כִּֽי־נְתָנָ֥ם יְהֹוָ֖ה בְּיַ֥ד יִשְׂרָאֵֽל׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the men of the garrison spoke to Jonathan, and to his armourbearer, and said: Come up to us, and we will shew you a thing. And Jonathan said to his armourbearer: Let us go up, follow me: for the Lord hath delivered them into the hands of Israel.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja miehet leiristä vastasivat Jonatania ja hänen aseensa kantajaa ja sanoivat: tulkaat tänne ylös meidän tykömme, me kyllä opetamme teitä. Niin sanoi Jonatan aseensa kantajalle: astu ylös minun jälkeeni, Herra on antanut heidät Israelin käsiin.
verses.king_james: And the men of the garrison answered Jonathan and his armourbearer, and said, Come up to us, and we will shew you a thing. And Jonathan said unto his armourbearer, Come up after me: for the Lord hath delivered them into the hand of Israel.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ascendit autem Jonathas manibus et pedibus reptans, et armiger ejus post eum. Itaque alii cadebant ante Jonathan, alios armiger ejus interficiebat sequens eum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּ֣עַל יוֹנָתָ֗ן עַל־יָדָיו֙ וְעַל־רַגְלָ֔יו וְנֹשֵׂ֥א כֵלָ֖יו אַחֲרָ֑יו וַֽיִּפְּלוּ֙ לִפְנֵ֣י יוֹנָתָ֔ן וְנֹשֵׂ֥א כֵלָ֖יו מְמוֹתֵ֥ת אַחֲרָֽיו׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Jonathan went up creeping on his hands and feet, and his armourbearer after him. And some fell before Jonathan, others his armourbearer slew as he followed him.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Jonatan kiipesi käsillänsä ja jaloillansa ylöspäin ja hänen aseensa kantaja hänen jälissänsä; ja he lankesivat maahan Jonatanin eteen ja hänen aseensa kantajan, joka löi heitä kuoliaaksi hänen jäljissänsä;
verses.king_james: And Jonathan climbed up upon his hands and upon his feet, and his armourbearer after him: and they fell before Jonathan; and his armourbearer slew after him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et facta est plaga prima qua percussit Jonathas et armiger ejus, quasi viginti virorum in media parte jugeri quam par boum in die arare consuevit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתְּהִ֞י הַמַּכָּ֣ה הָרִאשֹׁנָ֗ה אֲשֶׁ֨ר הִכָּ֧ה יוֹנָתָ֛ן וְנֹשֵׂ֥א כֵלָ֖יו כְּעֶשְׂרִ֣ים אִ֑ישׁ כְּבַחֲצִ֥י מַעֲנָ֖ה צֶ֥מֶד שָׂדֶֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the first slaughter which Jonathan and his armourbearer made, was of about twenty men, within half an acre of land, which a yoke of oxen is wont to plough in a day.
verses.finnish_biblia: Että ensimäinen tappelus oli, jona Jonatan ja hänen aseensa kantaja löi liki kaksikymmentä miestä, liki puolella vakomitalla peltoa, kuin juhtapari kyntäis.
verses.king_james: And that first slaughter, which Jonathan and his armourbearer made, was about twenty men, within as it were an half acre of land, which a yoke of oxen might plow.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et factum est miraculum in castris per agros : sed et omnis populus stationis eorum qui ierant ad prædandum, obstupuit, et conturbata est terra : et accidit quasi miraculum a Deo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתְּהִי֩ חֲרָדָ֨ה בַמַּחֲנֶ֤ה בַשָּׂדֶה֙ וּבְכׇל־הָעָ֔ם הַמַּצָּב֙ וְהַמַּשְׁחִ֔ית חָרְד֖וּ גַּם־הֵ֑מָּה וַתִּרְגַּ֣ז הָאָ֔רֶץ וַתְּהִ֖י לְחֶרְדַּ֥ת אֱלֹהִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: And there was a miracle in the camp, in the fields: and all the people of their garrison, who had gone out to plunder, were amazed, and the earth trembled: and it happened as a miracle from God.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja pelko tuli leiriin, kedolle ja koko kansan sekaan leirissä, ja hävittäjät myös peljätettiin, että maa vapisi siitä; sillä se hämmästys oli Jumalalta.
verses.king_james: And there was trembling in the host, in the field, and among all the people: the garrison, and the spoilers, they also trembled, and the earth quaked: so it was a very great trembling.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et respexerunt speculatores Saul qui erant in Gabaa Benjamin, et ecce multitudo prostrata, et huc illucque diffugiens.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּרְא֤וּ הַצֹּפִים֙ לְשָׁא֔וּל בְּגִבְעַ֖ת בִּנְיָמִ֑ן וְהִנֵּ֧ה הֶהָמ֛וֹן נָמ֖וֹג וַיֵּ֥לֶךְ וַהֲלֹֽם׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: And the watchmen of Saul, who were in Gabaa of Benjamin looked, and behold a multitude overthrown, and fleeing this way and that.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Saulin vartiat BenJaminin Gibeassa saivat nähdä kansan joukot hajoovan ja pakenevan, ja toinen toistansa sysivän,
verses.king_james: And the watchmen of Saul in Gibeah of Benjamin looked; and, behold, the multitude melted away, and they went on beating down one another.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ait Saul populo qui erat cum eo : Requirite, et videte quis abierit ex nobis. Cumque requisissent, repertum est non adesse Jonathan et armigerum ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֣אמֶר שָׁא֗וּל לָעָם֙ אֲשֶׁ֣ר אִתּ֔וֹ פִּקְדוּ־נָ֣א וּרְא֔וּ מִ֖י הָלַ֣ךְ מֵעִמָּ֑נוּ וַֽיִּפְקְד֔וּ וְהִנֵּ֛ה אֵ֥ין יוֹנָתָ֖ן וְנֹשֵׂ֥א כֵלָֽיו׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Saul said to the people that were with him: Look, and see who is gone from us. And when they had sought, it was found that Jonathan and his armourbearer were not there.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin sanoi Saul kansalle, joka hänen kanssansa oli: lukekaat nyt ja katsokaat, kuka meistä on mennyt pois; ja kuin he lukivat, katso, niin ei ollut Jonatan ja hänen aseensa kantaja siellä.
verses.king_james: Then said Saul unto the people that were with him, Number now, and see who is gone from us. And when they had numbered, behold, Jonathan and his armourbearer were not there.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ait Saul ad Achiam : Applica arcam Dei. (Erat enim ibi arca Dei in die illa cum filiis Israël.)
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֤אמֶר שָׁאוּל֙ לַֽאֲחִיָּ֔ה הַגִּ֖ישָׁה אֲר֣וֹן הָאֱלֹהִ֑ים כִּֽי־הָיָ֞ה אֲר֧וֹן הָאֱלֹהִ֛ים בַּיּ֥וֹם הַה֖וּא וּבְנֵ֥י יִשְׂרָאֵֽל׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Saul said to Achias: Bring the ark of the Lord. (For the ark of God was there that day with the children of Israel.)
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Saul sanoi Ahialle: tuo tänne Jumalan arkki; (sillä Jumalan arkki oli siihen aikaan Israelin lasten tykönä.)
verses.king_james: And Saul said unto Ahiah, Bring hither the ark of God. For the ark of God was at that time with the children of Israel.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cumque loqueretur Saul ad sacerdotem, tumultus magnus exortus est in castris Philisthinorum : crescebatque paulatim, et clarius resonabat. Et ait Saul ad sacerdotem : Contrahe manum tuam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְהִ֗י עַ֣ד דִּבֶּ֤ר שָׁאוּל֙ אֶל־הַכֹּהֵ֔ן וְהֶהָמ֗וֹן אֲשֶׁר֙ בְּמַחֲנֵ֣ה פְלִשְׁתִּ֔ים וַיֵּ֥לֶךְ הָל֖וֹךְ וָרָ֑ב {פ}וַיֹּ֧אמֶר שָׁא֛וּל אֶל־הַכֹּהֵ֖ן אֱסֹ֥ף יָדֶֽךָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And while Saul spoke to the priest, there arose a great uproar in the camp of the Philistines: and it increased by degrees, and was heard more clearly. And Saul said to the priest: Draw in thy hand.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kuin Saul vielä puhui papin kanssa, eneni kapina ja juoksu Philistealaisten leirissä. Ja Saul sanoi papille: ota kätes pois.
verses.king_james: And it came to pass, while Saul talked unto the priest, that the noise that was in the host of the Philistines went on and increased: and Saul said unto the priest, Withdraw thine hand.

verses.latin_vulgate: Conclamavit ergo Saul, et omnis populus qui erat cum eo, et venerunt usque ad locum certaminis : et ecce versus fuerat gladius uniuscujusque ad proximum suum, et cædes magna nimis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּזָּעֵ֣ק שָׁא֗וּל וְכׇל־הָעָם֙ אֲשֶׁ֣ר אִתּ֔וֹ וַיָּבֹ֖אוּ עַד־הַמִּלְחָמָ֑ה וְהִנֵּ֨ה הָיְתָ֜ה חֶ֤רֶב אִישׁ֙ בְּרֵעֵ֔הוּ מְהוּמָ֖ה גְּדוֹלָ֥ה מְאֹֽד׃
verses.douay_rheims: Then Saul, and all the people that were with him, shouted together, and they came to the place of the fight: and behold every man's sword was turned upon his neighbour, and there was a very great slaughter.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Saul kokoontui ja kaikki kansa joka hänen kanssansa oli, ja tulivat sotaan. Ja katso, silloin kävi itsekunkin miekka lähimmäistänsä vastaan, ja oli sangen suuri kapina.
verses.king_james: And Saul and all the people that were with him assembled themselves, and they came to the battle: and, behold, every man’s sword was against his fellow, and there was a very great discomfiture.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sed et Hebræi qui fuerant cum Philisthiim heri et nudiustertius, ascenderantque cum eis in castris, reversi sunt ut essent cum Israël qui erant cum Saul et Jonatha.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהָעִבְרִ֗ים הָי֤וּ לַפְּלִשְׁתִּים֙ כְּאֶתְמ֣וֹל שִׁלְשׁ֔וֹם אֲשֶׁ֨ר עָל֥וּ עִמָּ֛ם בַּֽמַּחֲנֶ֖ה סָבִ֑יב וְגַם־הֵ֗מָּה לִֽהְיוֹת֙ עִם־יִשְׂרָאֵ֔ל אֲשֶׁ֥ר עִם־שָׁא֖וּל וְיוֹנָתָֽן׃
verses.douay_rheims: Moreover, the Hebrews that had been with the Philistines yesterday and the day before, and went up with them into the camp, returned to be with the Israelites, who were with Saul and Jonathan.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja ne Hebrealaiset, jotka ennen olivat olleet Philistealaisten tykönä ja olivat käyneet heidän kanssansa leirin ympäri, antoivat heitänsä Israelin sekaan, jotka olivat Saulin ja Jonatanin kanssa.
verses.king_james: Moreover the Hebrews that were with the Philistines before that time, which went up with them into the camp from the country round about, even they also turned to be with the Israelites that were with Saul and Jonathan.

verses.latin_vulgate: Omnes quoque Israëlitæ qui se absconderant in monte Ephraim, audientes quod fugissent Philisthæi, sociaverunt se cum suis in prælio. Et erant cum Saul quasi decem millia virorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְכֹל֩ אִ֨ישׁ יִשְׂרָאֵ֜ל הַמִּֽתְחַבְּאִ֤ים בְּהַר־אֶפְרַ֙יִם֙ שָֽׁמְע֔וּ כִּי־נָ֖סוּ פְּלִשְׁתִּ֑ים וַֽיַּדְבְּק֥וּ גַם־הֵ֛מָּה אַחֲרֵיהֶ֖ם בַּמִּלְחָמָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And all the Israelites that had hid themselves in mount Ephraim, hearing that the Philistines fled, joined themselves with their countrymen in the fight. And there were with Saul about ten thousand men.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kaikki Israelin miehet, jotka heitänsä kätkeneet olivat Ephraimin vuorelle, kuin he kuulivat Philistealaisten paenneen, ajoivat he myös heitä takaa sodassa.
verses.king_james: Likewise all the men of Israel which had hid themselves in mount Ephraim, when they heard that the Philistines fled, even they also followed hard after them in the battle.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et salvavit Dominus in die illa Israël : pugna autem pervenit usque ad Bethaven.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיּ֧וֹשַׁע יְהֹוָ֛ה בַּיּ֥וֹם הַה֖וּא אֶת־יִשְׂרָאֵ֑ל וְהַ֨מִּלְחָמָ֔ה עָבְרָ֖ה אֶת־בֵּ֥ית אָֽוֶן׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the Lord saved Israel that day. And the fight went on as far as Bethaven.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja niin Herra autti silloin Israelia; ja sota seisoi BetAveniin asti.
verses.king_james: So the Lord saved Israel that day: and the battle passed over unto Beth–aven.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et viri Israël sociati sunt sibi in die illa : adjuravit autem Saul populum, dicens : Maledictus vir qui comederit panem usque ad vesperam, donec ulciscar de inimicis meis. Et non manducavit universus populus panem :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאִֽישׁ־יִשְׂרָאֵ֥ל נִגַּ֖שׂ בַּיּ֣וֹם הַה֑וּא וַיֹּ֩אֶל֩ שָׁא֨וּל אֶת־הָעָ֜ם לֵאמֹ֗ר אָר֣וּר הָ֠אִ֠ישׁ אֲשֶׁר־יֹ֨אכַל לֶ֜חֶם עַד־הָעֶ֗רֶב וְנִקַּמְתִּי֙ מֵאֹ֣יְבַ֔י וְלֹא־טָעַ֥ם כׇּל־הָעָ֖ם לָֽחֶם׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: And the men of Israel were joined together that day: and Saul adjured the people, saying: Cursed be the man that shall eat food till evening, till I be revenged of my enemies. So none of the people tasted any food.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kuin Israelin miehet olivat väsyneet sinä päivänä, vannotti Saul kaiken kansan, ja sanoi: kirottu olkoon jokainen, joka syö jotakin ehtooseen asti, että minä kostaisin vihollisilleni. Ja koko kansa ei syönyt mitäkään.
verses.king_james: And the men of Israel were distressed that day: for Saul had adjured the people, saying, Cursed be the man that eateth any food until evening, that I may be avenged on mine enemies. So none of the people tasted any food.

verses.latin_vulgate: omneque terræ vulgus venit in saltum, in quo erat mel super faciem agri.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְכׇל־הָאָ֖רֶץ בָּ֣אוּ בַיָּ֑עַר וַיְהִ֥י דְבַ֖שׁ עַל־פְּנֵ֥י הַשָּׂדֶֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And all the common people came into a forest, in which there was honey upon the ground.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kaikki kansa tuli metsään, ja siellä oli hunajaa kedolla.
verses.king_james: And all they of the land came to a wood; and there was honey upon the ground.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ingressus est itaque populus saltum, et apparuit fluens mel, nullusque applicuit manum ad os suum : timebat enim populus juramentum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּבֹ֤א הָעָם֙ אֶל־הַיַּ֔עַר וְהִנֵּ֖ה הֵ֣לֶךְ דְּבָ֑שׁ וְאֵין־מַשִּׂ֤יג יָדוֹ֙ אֶל־פִּ֔יו כִּֽי־יָרֵ֥א הָעָ֖ם אֶת־הַשְּׁבֻעָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And when the people came into the forest, behold the honey dropped, but no man put his hand to his mouth. For the people feared the oath.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuin kansa tuli metsään, katso, silloin vuosi siellä hunajaa, mutta ei yksikään ottanut sitä kädellänsä suuhunsa; sillä kansa pelkäsi valaa.
verses.king_james: And when the people were come into the wood, behold, the honey dropped; but no man put his hand to his mouth: for the people feared the oath.

verses.latin_vulgate: Porro Jonathas non audierat cum adjuraret pater ejus populum : extenditque summitatem virgæ quam habebat in manu, et intinxit in favum mellis : et convertit manum suam ad os suum, et illuminati sunt oculi ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְיוֹנָתָ֣ן לֹא־שָׁמַ֗ע בְּהַשְׁבִּ֣יעַ אָבִיו֮ אֶת־הָעָם֒ וַיִּשְׁלַ֗ח אֶת־קְצֵ֤ה הַמַּטֶּה֙ אֲשֶׁ֣ר בְּיָד֔וֹ וַיִּטְבֹּ֥ל אוֹתָ֖הּ בְּיַעְרַ֣ת הַדְּבָ֑שׁ וַיָּ֤שֶׁב יָדוֹ֙ אֶל־פִּ֔יו (ותראנה) [וַתָּאֹ֖רְנָה] עֵינָֽיו׃
verses.douay_rheims: But Jonathan had not heard when his father adjured the people: and he put forth the end of the rod, which he had in his hand, and dipt it in a honeycomb: and he carried his hand to his mouth, and his eyes were enlightened.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta Jonatan ei kuullut isänsä vannottaneeksi kansaa, hän ojensi sauvansa joka hänen kädessänsä oli, ja satutti pään hunajaläjään, ja pisti kätensä suuhunsa, niin hänen silmänsä valpauntuivat.
verses.king_james: But Jonathan heard not when his father charged the people with the oath: wherefore he put forth the end of the rod that was in his hand, and dipped it in an honeycomb, and put his hand to his mouth; and his eyes were enlightened.

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondensque unus de populo, ait : Jurejurando constrinxit pater tuus populum, dicens : Maledictus vir qui comederit panem hodie. (Defecerat autem populus.)
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּ֩עַן֩ אִ֨ישׁ מֵהָעָ֜ם וַיֹּ֗אמֶר הַשְׁבֵּ֩עַ֩ הִשְׁבִּ֨יעַ אָבִ֤יךָ אֶת־הָעָם֙ לֵאמֹ֔ר אָר֥וּר הָאִ֛ישׁ אֲשֶׁר־יֹ֥אכַל לֶ֖חֶם הַיּ֑וֹם וַיָּ֖עַף הָעָֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And one of the people answering, said: Thy father hath bound the people with an oath, saying: Cursed be the man that shall eat any food this day. (And the people were faint.)
verses.finnish_biblia: Silloin vastasi yksi kansasta ja sanoi: isäs vannotti kovasti kansaa, ja sanoi: kirottu olkoon jokainen, joka jotakin syö tänäpänä. Ja kansa oli väsyksissä.
verses.king_james: Then answered one of the people, and said, Thy father straitly charged the people with an oath, saying, Cursed be the man that eateth any food this day. And the people were faint.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixitque Jonathas : Turbavit pater meus terram : vidistis ipsi quia illuminati sunt oculi mei, eo quod gustaverim paululum de melle isto :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֙אמֶר֙ יֽוֹנָתָ֔ן עָכַ֥ר אָבִ֖י אֶת־הָאָ֑רֶץ רְאוּ־נָא֙ כִּי־אֹ֣רוּ עֵינַ֔י כִּ֣י טָעַ֔מְתִּי מְעַ֖ט דְּבַ֥שׁ הַזֶּֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Jonathan said: My father hath troubled the land: you have seen yourselves that my eyes are enlightened, because I tasted a little of this honey:
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Jonatan sanoi: minun isäni on turmellut maan: katsos, kuinka minun silmäni valpauntuivat, että minä tästä jotakin maistoin.
verses.king_james: Then said Jonathan, My father hath troubled the land: see, I pray you, how mine eyes have been enlightened, because I tasted a little of this honey.

verses.latin_vulgate: quanto magis si comedisset populus de præda inimicorum suorum, quam reperit ? nonne major plaga facta fuisset in Philisthiim ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַ֗ף כִּ֡י לוּא֩ אָכֹ֨ל אָכַ֤ל הַיּוֹם֙ הָעָ֔ם מִשְּׁלַ֥ל אֹיְבָ֖יו אֲשֶׁ֣ר מָצָ֑א כִּ֥י עַתָּ֛ה לֹא־רָבְתָ֥ה מַכָּ֖ה בַּפְּלִשְׁתִּֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: How much more if the people had eaten of the prey of their enemies, which they found? had there not been made a greater slaughter among the Philistines?
verses.finnish_biblia: Jos kansa tänäpänä olis syönyt vihollistensa saalista, kuin he löytäneet ovat, niin olis tappelus ollut suurempi Philistealaisia vastaan.
verses.king_james: How much more, if haply the people had eaten freely to day of the spoil of their enemies which they found? for had there not been now a much greater slaughter among the Philistines?

verses.latin_vulgate: Percusserunt ergo in die illa Philisthæos a Machmis usque in Ajalon.   Defatigatus est autem populus nimis :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּכּ֞וּ בַּיּ֤וֹם הַהוּא֙ בַּפְּלִשְׁתִּ֔ים מִמִּכְמָ֖שׂ אַיָּלֹ֑נָה וַיָּ֥עַף הָעָ֖ם מְאֹֽד׃
verses.douay_rheims: So they smote that day the Philistines, from Machmas to Aialon. And the people were wearied exceedingly.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja he löivät sinä päivänä Philistealaisia Mikmasta Ajaloniin asti; ja kansa väsyi sangen kovin.
verses.king_james: And they smote the Philistines that day from Michmash to Aijalon: and the people were very faint.

verses.latin_vulgate: et versus ad prædam tulit oves, et boves, et vitulos, et mactaverunt in terra : comeditque populus cum sanguine.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: (ויעש) [וַיַּ֤עַט] הָעָם֙ אֶל־[הַשָּׁלָ֔ל] (שלל) וַיִּקְח֨וּ צֹ֧אן וּבָקָ֛ר וּבְנֵ֥י בָקָ֖ר וַיִּשְׁחֲטוּ־אָ֑רְצָה וַיֹּ֥אכַל הָעָ֖ם עַל־הַדָּֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And falling upon the spoils, they took sheep, and oxen, and calves, and slew them on the ground: and the people ate them with the blood.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kansa jakoi saaliin, ja ottivat lampaita, ja karjaa, ja vasikoita, ja teurastivat maan päällä, ja söivät ne verinensä.
verses.king_james: And the people flew upon the spoil, and took sheep, and oxen, and calves, and slew them on the ground: and the people did eat them with the blood.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nuntiaverunt autem Sauli dicentes quod populus peccasset Domino, comedens cum sanguine. Qui ait : Prævaricati estis : volvite ad me jam nunc saxum grande.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּגִּ֤ידוּ לְשָׁאוּל֙ לֵאמֹ֔ר הִנֵּ֥ה הָעָ֛ם חֹטִ֥אים לַיהֹוָ֖ה לֶאֱכֹ֣ל עַל־הַדָּ֑ם וַיֹּ֣אמֶר בְּגַדְתֶּ֔ם גֹּלּוּ־אֵלַ֥י הַיּ֖וֹם אֶ֥בֶן גְּדוֹלָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And they told Saul that the people had sinned against the Lord, eating with the blood. And he said: You have transgressed: roll here to me now a great stone.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin Saulille ilmoitettiin sanoen: katso, kansa on syntiä tehnyt Herraa vastaan ja syönyt verinensä. Hän sanoi: te olette pahasti tehneet: vierittäkäät nyt minulle tänne suuri kivi.
verses.king_james: Then they told Saul, saying, Behold, the people sin against the Lord, in that they eat with the blood. And he said, Ye have transgressed: roll a great stone unto me this day.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dixit Saul : Dispergimini in vulgus, et dicite eis ut adducat ad me unusquisque bovem suum et arietem, et occidite super istud, et vescimini, et non peccabitis Domino comedentes cum sanguine. Adduxit itaque omnis populus unusquisque bovem in manu sua usque ad noctem : et occiderunt ibi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֣אמֶר שָׁא֣וּל פֻּ֣צוּ בָעָ֡ם וַאֲמַרְתֶּ֣ם לָהֶ֡ם הַגִּ֣ישׁוּ אֵלַי֩ אִ֨ישׁ שׁוֹר֜וֹ וְאִ֣ישׁ שְׂיֵ֗הוּ וּשְׁחַטְתֶּ֤ם בָּזֶה֙ וַאֲכַלְתֶּ֔ם וְלֹא־תֶחֶטְא֥וּ לַיהֹוָ֖ה לֶאֱכֹ֣ל אֶל־הַדָּ֑ם וַיַּגִּ֨שׁוּ כׇל־הָעָ֜ם אִ֣ישׁ שׁוֹר֧וֹ בְיָד֛וֹ הַלַּ֖יְלָה וַיִּשְׁחֲטוּ־שָֽׁם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Saul said: Disperse yourselves among the people, and tell them to bring me every man his ox and his ram and slay them upon this stone, and eat, and you shall not sin against the Lord, in eating with the blood. So all the people brought every man his ox with him till the night: and slew them there.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Saul sanoi: menkäät ulos kansan sekaan ja sanokaat heille: tuokaan itsekukin härkänsä ja lampaansa minun tyköni ja teurastakaan tässä, että te söisitte ja ette syntiä tekisi Herraa vastaan veren syömisellä; niin kaikki kansa toivat kukin härkänsä kädessänsä yöllä ja teurastivat ne siellä.
verses.king_james: And Saul said, Disperse yourselves among the people, and say unto them, Bring me hither every man his ox, and every man his sheep, and slay them here, and eat; and sin not against the Lord in eating with the blood. And all the people brought every man his ox with him that night, and slew them there.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ædificavit autem Saul altare Domino, tuncque primum cœpit ædificare altare Domino.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּ֧בֶן שָׁא֛וּל מִזְבֵּ֖חַ לַיהֹוָ֑ה אֹת֣וֹ הֵחֵ֔ל לִבְנ֥וֹת מִזְבֵּ֖חַ לַיהֹוָֽה׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: And Saul built an altar to the Lord: and he then first began to build an altar to the Lord.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Saul rakensi Herralle alttarin: se on ensimäinen alttari, jonka hän rakensi Herralle.
verses.king_james: And Saul built an altar unto the Lord: the same was the first altar that he built unto the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dixit Saul : Irruamus super Philisthæos nocte, et vastemus eos usque dum illucescat mane, nec relinquamus ex eis virum. Dixitque populus : Omne quod bonum videtur in oculis tuis, fac. Et ait sacerdos : Accedamus huc ad Deum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֣אמֶר שָׁא֡וּל נֵרְדָ֣ה אַחֲרֵי֩ פְלִשְׁתִּ֨ים ׀ לַ֜יְלָה וְֽנָבֹ֥זָה בָהֶ֣ם ׀ עַד־א֣וֹר הַבֹּ֗קֶר וְלֹֽא־נַשְׁאֵ֤ר בָּהֶם֙ אִ֔ישׁ וַיֹּ֣אמְר֔וּ כׇּל־הַטּ֥וֹב בְּעֵינֶ֖יךָ עֲשֵׂ֑ה {ס} וַיֹּ֙אמֶר֙ הַכֹּהֵ֔ן נִקְרְבָ֥ה הֲלֹ֖ם אֶל־הָאֱלֹהִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Saul said: Let us fall upon the Philistines by night, and destroy them till the morning light, and let us not leave a man of them. And the people said: Do all that seemeth good in thy eyes. And the priest said: Let us draw near hither unto God.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Saul sanoi: käykäämme alas Philistealaisten jälkeen yöllä ja ryöstäkäämme heitä, siihenasti kuin päivä valkenee, ettemme yhtäkään heistä jättäisi. He vastasivat: tee kaikki mitä sinulle kelpaa. Ja pappi sanoi: käykäämme tänne Jumalan tykö.
verses.king_james: And Saul said, Let us go down after the Philistines by night, and spoil them until the morning light, and let us not leave a man of them. And they said, Do whatsoever seemeth good unto thee. Then said the priest, Let us draw near hither unto God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et consuluit Saul Dominum : Num persequar Philisthiim ? si trades eos in manus Israël ? Et non respondit ei in die illa.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשְׁאַ֤ל שָׁאוּל֙ בֵּֽאלֹהִ֔ים הַֽאֵרֵד֙ אַחֲרֵ֣י פְלִשְׁתִּ֔ים הֲתִתְּנֵ֖ם בְּיַ֣ד יִשְׂרָאֵ֑ל וְלֹ֥א עָנָ֖הוּ בַּיּ֥וֹם הַהֽוּא׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Saul consulted the Lord: Shall I pursue after the Philistines? wilt thou deliver them into the hands of Israel? And he answered him not that day.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Saul kysyi Jumalalta ja sanoi: astunko minä Philistealaisten perään alas, ja annatkos heitä Israelin käsiin? Mutta ei hän häntä silloin vastannut.
verses.king_james: And Saul asked counsel of God, Shall I go down after the Philistines? wilt thou deliver them into the hand of Israel? But he answered him not that day.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixitque Saul : Applicate huc universos angulos populi : et scitote, et videte per quem acciderit peccatum hoc hodie.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֣אמֶר שָׁא֔וּל גֹּ֣שֽׁוּ הֲלֹ֔ם כֹּ֖ל פִּנּ֣וֹת הָעָ֑ם וּדְע֣וּ וּרְא֔וּ בַּמָּ֗ה הָ֥יְתָ֛ה הַחַטָּ֥את הַזֹּ֖את הַיּֽוֹם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Saul said: Bring hither all the corners of the people: and know, and see by whom this sin hath happened to day.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin sanoi Saul: tulkaan väki tänne joka kulmalta, tiedustelkaat ja katselkaat, kenessä tänäpänä synti lienee.
verses.king_james: And Saul said, Draw ye near hither, all the chief of the people: and know and see wherein this sin hath been this day.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vivit Dominus salvator Israël, quia si per Jonathan filium meum factum est, absque retractione morietur. Ad quod nullus contradixit ei de omni populo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֣י חַי־יְהֹוָ֗ה הַמּוֹשִׁ֙יעַ֙ אֶת־יִשְׂרָאֵ֔ל כִּ֧י אִם־יֶשְׁנ֛וֹ בְּיוֹנָתָ֥ן בְּנִ֖י כִּ֣י מ֣וֹת יָמ֑וּת וְאֵ֥ין עֹנֵ֖הוּ מִכׇּל־הָעָֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: As the Lord liveth, who is the Saviour of Israel, if it was done by Jonathan, my son, he shall surely die. In this none of the people gainsayed him.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sillä, niin totta kuin Herra Israelin auttaja elää, vaikka se olis minun pojassani Jonatanissa, niin hänen pitää totisesti kuoleman. Ja ei yksikään vastannut häntä kaikesta kansasta.
verses.king_james: For, as the Lord liveth, which saveth Israel, though it be in Jonathan my son, he shall surely die. But there was not a man among all the people that answered him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ait ad universum Israël : Separamini vos in partem unam, et ego cum Jonatha filio meo ero in parte altera. Responditque populus ad Saul : Quod bonum videtur in oculis tuis, fac.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֣אמֶר אֶל־כׇּל־יִשְׂרָאֵ֗ל אַתֶּם֙ תִּֽהְיוּ֙ לְעֵ֣בֶר אֶחָ֔ד וַֽאֲנִי֙ וְיוֹנָתָ֣ן בְּנִ֔י נִהְיֶ֖ה לְעֵ֣בֶר אֶחָ֑ד וַיֹּאמְר֤וּ הָעָם֙ אֶל־שָׁא֔וּל הַטּ֥וֹב בְּעֵינֶ֖יךָ עֲשֵֽׂה׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: And he said to all Israel: Be you on one side and I, with Jonathan, my son, will be on the other side. And the people answered Saul: Do what seemeth good in thy eyes.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hän sanoi koko Israelille: olkaat te sillä puolelle, minä ja Jonatan minun poikani olemme tällä puolella. Kansa sanoi Saulille: tee kuin sinulle kelpaa.
verses.king_james: Then said he unto all Israel, Be ye on one side, and I and Jonathan my son will be on the other side. And the people said unto Saul, Do what seemeth good unto thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dixit Saul ad Dominum Deum Israël : Domine Deus Israël, da indicium : quid est quod non responderis servo tuo hodie ? si in me, aut in Jonatha filio meo, est iniquitas hæc, da ostensionem : aut si hæc iniquitas est in populo tuo, da sanctitatem. Et deprehensus est Jonathas et Saul : populus autem exivit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֣אמֶר שָׁא֗וּל אֶל־יְהֹוָ֛ה אֱלֹהֵ֥י יִשְׂרָאֵ֖ל הָ֣בָה תָמִ֑ים וַיִּלָּכֵ֧ד יוֹנָתָ֛ן וְשָׁא֖וּל וְהָעָ֥ם יָצָֽאוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Saul said to the Lord: O Lord God of Israel, give a sign, by which we may know, what the meaning is, that thou answerest not thy servant to day: If this iniquity be in me, or in my son Jonathan, give a proof: or if this iniquity be in thy people, give holiness. And Jonathan and Saul were taken, and the people escaped.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Saul sanoi Herralle Israelin Jumalalle: tee oikeus; niin se osasi Jonatanin ja Saulin, ja kansa meni vapaana ulos.
verses.king_james: Therefore Saul said unto the Lord God of Israel, Give a perfect lot. And Saul and Jonathan were taken: but the people escaped.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ait Saul : Mittite sortem inter me et inter Jonathan filium meum. Et captus est Jonathas.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֣אמֶר שָׁא֔וּל הַפִּ֕ילוּ בֵּינִ֕י וּבֵ֖ין יוֹנָתָ֣ן בְּנִ֑י וַיִּלָּכֵ֖ד יוֹנָתָֽן׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Saul said: (Cast lots between me, and Jonathan, my son. And Jonathan was taken.
verses.finnish_biblia: Saul sanoi: heittäkäät minusta ja minun pojastani Jonatanista; niin se lankesi Jonataniin.
verses.king_james: And Saul said, Cast lots between me and Jonathan my son. And Jonathan was taken.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixit autem Saul ad Jonathan : Indica mihi quid feceris. Et indicavit ei Jonathas, et ait : Gustans gustavi in summitate virgæ quæ erat in manu mea, paululum mellis, et ecce ego morior.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֤אמֶר שָׁאוּל֙ אֶל־י֣וֹנָתָ֔ן הַגִּ֥ידָה לִּ֖י מֶ֣ה עָשִׂ֑יתָה וַיַּגֶּד־ל֣וֹ יוֹנָתָ֗ן וַיֹּ֩אמֶר֩ טָעֹ֨ם טָעַ֜מְתִּי בִּקְצֵ֨ה הַמַּטֶּ֧ה אֲשֶׁר־בְּיָדִ֛י מְעַ֥ט דְּבַ֖שׁ הִנְנִ֥י אָמֽוּת׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: And Saul said to Jonathan: Tell me what thou hast done. And Jonathan told him, and said: I did but taste a little honey with the end of the rod, which was in my hand, and behold I must die.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Saul sanoi Jonatanille: ilmoita minulle, mitäs olet tehnyt? Jonatan ilmoitti hänelle ja sanoi: tosin minä maistoin vähän hunajaa sauvani nenällä joka minun kädessäni oli, ja katso minun pitää sentähden kuoleman?
verses.king_james: Then Saul said to Jonathan, Tell me what thou hast done. And Jonathan told him, and said, I did but taste a little honey with the end of the rod that was in mine hand, and, lo, I must die.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ait Saul : Hæc faciat mihi Deus, et hæc addat, quia morte morieris, Jonatha.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֣אמֶר שָׁא֔וּל כֹּה־יַעֲשֶׂ֥ה אֱלֹהִ֖ים וְכֹ֣ה יוֹסִ֑ף כִּי־מ֥וֹת תָּמ֖וּת יוֹנָתָֽן׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Saul said: May God do so and so to me, and add still more: for dying thou shalt die, O Jonathan.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Saul sanoi: Jumala tehköön minulle niin ja niin! Jonatan, sinun pitää totisesti kuoleman!
verses.king_james: And Saul answered, God do so and more also: for thou shalt surely die, Jonathan.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixitque populus ad Saul : Ergone Jonathas morietur, qui fecit salutem hanc magnam in Israël ? hoc nefas est : vivit Dominus, si ceciderit capillus de capite ejus in terram, quia cum Deo operatus est hodie. Liberavit ergo populus Jonathan, ut non moreretur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֨אמֶר הָעָ֜ם אֶל־שָׁא֗וּל הֲֽיוֹנָתָ֤ן ׀ יָמוּת֙ אֲשֶׁ֣ר עָ֠שָׂ֠ה הַיְשׁוּעָ֨ה הַגְּדוֹלָ֣ה הַזֹּאת֮ בְּיִשְׂרָאֵל֒ חָלִ֗ילָה חַי־יְהֹוָה֙ אִם־יִפֹּ֞ל מִשַּׂעֲרַ֤ת רֹאשׁוֹ֙ אַ֔רְצָה כִּֽי־עִם־אֱלֹהִ֥ים עָשָׂ֖ה הַיּ֣וֹם הַזֶּ֑ה וַיִּפְדּ֥וּ הָעָ֛ם אֶת־יוֹנָתָ֖ן וְלֹא־מֵֽת׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: And the people said to Saul: Shall Jonathan then die, who hath wrought this great salvation in Israel? this must not be: As the Lord liveth, there shall not one hair of his head fall to the ground, for he hath wrought with God this day. So the people delivered Jonathan, that he should not die.
verses.finnish_biblia: Vaan kansa sanoi Saulille: pitääkö Jonatanin kuoleman, joka tämän suuren autuuden teki Israelissa? Pois se: niin totta kuin Herra elää, ei hiuskarvakaan pidä hänen päästänsä putooman: sillä Jumala on sen tehnyt tänäpänä hänen kauttansa. Ja näin kansa vapahti Jonatanin kuolemasta.
verses.king_james: And the people said unto Saul, Shall Jonathan die, who hath wrought this great salvation in Israel? God forbid: as the Lord liveth, there shall not one hair of his head fall to the ground; for he hath wrought with God this day. So the people rescued Jonathan, that he died not.

verses.latin_vulgate: Recessitque Saul, nec persecutus est Philisthiim : porro Philisthiim abierunt in loca sua.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּ֣עַל שָׁא֔וּל מֵאַחֲרֵ֖י פְּלִשְׁתִּ֑ים וּפְלִשְׁתִּ֖ים הָלְכ֥וּ לִמְקוֹמָֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Saul went back, and did not pursue after the Philistines: and the Philistines went to their own places.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin Saul meni Philistealaisten tyköä pois; ja Philistealaiset menivät sioillensa.
verses.king_james: Then Saul went up from following the Philistines: and the Philistines went to their own place.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et Saul, confirmato regno super Israël, pugnabat per circuitum adversum omnes inimicos ejus, contra Moab, et filios Ammon, et Edom, et reges Soba, et Philisthæos : et quocumque se verterat, superabat.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְשָׁא֛וּל לָכַ֥ד הַמְּלוּכָ֖ה עַל־יִשְׂרָאֵ֑ל וַיִּלָּ֣חֶם סָבִ֣יב ׀ בְּֽכׇל־אֹיְבָ֡יו בְּמוֹאָ֣ב ׀ וּבִבְנֵֽי־עַמּ֨וֹן וּבֶאֱד֜וֹם וּבְמַלְכֵ֤י צוֹבָה֙ וּבַפְּלִשְׁתִּ֔ים וּבְכֹ֥ל אֲשֶׁר־יִפְנֶ֖ה יַרְשִֽׁיעַ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Saul having his kingdom established over Israel, fought against all his enemies round about, against Moab, and against the children of Ammon, and Edom, and the kings of Soba, and the Philistines: and whithersoever he turned himself, he overcame.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta kuin Saul oli saanut Israelin valtakunnan, soti hän joka taholta kaikkia vihollisiansa vastaan, Moabilaisia, Ammonilaisia, Edomilaisia, Zoban kuninkaita ja Philistealaisia vastaan; ja kuhunka hän ikänä hänensä käänsi, siellä hän rankaisi.
verses.king_james: So Saul took the kingdom over Israel, and fought against all his enemies on every side, against Moab, and against the children of Ammon, and against Edom, and against the kings of Zobah, and against the Philistines: and whithersoever he turned himself, he vexed them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Congregatoque exercitu, percussit Amalec, et eruit Israël de manu vastatorum ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּ֣עַשׂ חַ֔יִל וַיַּ֖ךְ אֶת־עֲמָלֵ֑ק וַיַּצֵּ֥ל אֶת־יִשְׂרָאֵ֖ל מִיַּ֥ד שֹׁסֵֽהוּ׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: And gathering together an army, he defeated Amalec, and delivered Israel from the hand of them that spoiled them.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hän teki urhoollisia töitä ja löi Amalekilaiset ja vapahti Israelin niiden käsistä, jotka häntä raatelivat.
verses.king_james: And he gathered an host, and smote the Amalekites, and delivered Israel out of the hands of them that spoiled them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Fuerunt autem filii Saul, Jonathas, et Jessui, et Melchisua : et nomina duarum filiarum ejus, nomen primogenitæ Merob, et nomen minoris Michol.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּֽהְיוּ֙ בְּנֵ֣י שָׁא֔וּל יוֹנָתָ֥ן וְיִשְׁוִ֖י וּמַלְכִּישׁ֑וּעַ וְשֵׁם֙ שְׁתֵּ֣י בְנֹתָ֔יו שֵׁ֤ם הַבְּכִירָה֙ מֵרַ֔ב וְשֵׁ֥ם הַקְּטַנָּ֖ה מִיכַֽל׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the sons of Saul, were Jonathan, and Jessui, and Melchisua: and the names of his two daughters, the name of the firstborn was Merob, and the name of the younger Michol.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Saulilla oli poikia: Jonatan, Isvi, Malkisua, ja hänen kahden tyttärensä nimet, esikoisen nimi Merab ja nuoremman nimi Mikal,
verses.king_james: Now the sons of Saul were Jonathan, and Ishui, and Melchi–shua: and the names of his two daughters were these; the name of the firstborn Merab, and the name of the younger Michal:

verses.latin_vulgate: Et nomen uxoris Saul Achinoam filia Achimaas : et nomen principis militiæ ejus Abner filius Ner, patruelis Saul.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְשֵׁם֙ אֵ֣שֶׁת שָׁא֔וּל אֲחִינֹ֖עַם בַּת־אֲחִימָ֑עַץ וְשֵׁ֤ם שַׂר־צְבָאוֹ֙ אֲבִינֵ֔ר בֶּן־נֵ֖ר דּ֥וֹד שָׁאֽוּל׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the name of Saul's wife was Achinoam, the daughter of Achimaas; and the name of the captain of his army was Abner, the son of Ner, the cousin german of Saul.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Saulin emännän nimi oli Ahinoam, Ahimatsin tytär, ja hänen sotajoukkonsa päämiehen nimi Abner, Nerin poika, Saulin sedän;
verses.king_james: And the name of Saul’s wife was Ahinoam, the daughter of Ahimaaz: and the name of the captain of his host was Abner, the son of Ner, Saul’s uncle.

verses.latin_vulgate: Porro Cis fuit pater Saul, et Ner pater Abner, filius Abiel.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְקִ֧ישׁ אֲבִֽי־שָׁא֛וּל וְנֵ֥ר אֲבִֽי־אַבְנֵ֖ר בֶּן־אֲבִיאֵֽל׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: For Cis was the father of Saul, and Ner, the father of Abner, was son of Abiel.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Kis Saulin isä, ja Ner Abnerin isä, oli Abielin poika.
verses.king_james: And Kish was the father of Saul; and Ner the father of Abner was the son of Abiel.

verses.latin_vulgate: Erat autem bellum potens adversum Philisthæos omnibus diebus Saul. Nam quemcumque viderat Saul virum fortem, et aptum ad prælium, sociabat eum sibi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתְּהִ֤י הַמִּלְחָמָה֙ חֲזָקָ֣ה עַל־פְּלִשְׁתִּ֔ים כֹּ֖ל יְמֵ֣י שָׁא֑וּל וְרָאָ֨ה שָׁא֜וּל כׇּל־אִ֤ישׁ גִּבּוֹר֙ וְכׇל־בֶּן־חַ֔יִל וַיַּאַסְפֵ֖הוּ אֵלָֽיו׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: And there was a great war against the Philistines all the days of Saul. For whomsoever Saul saw to be a valiant man, and fit for war, he took him to himself.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja suuri sota oli Philistealaisia vastaan niinkauvan kuin Saul eli. Ja kussa ikinä Saul näki jalon ja väkevän sotamiehen, otti hän sen tykönsä.
verses.king_james: And there was sore war against the Philistines all the days of Saul: and when Saul saw any strong man, or any valiant man, he took him unto him.
